Propositional logic refers to logic using [[propositions]]. It is not using mathematical variables, but uses statements and rules around the statements. Also known as propositional calculus


Basically propositional logic takes a list of premises or assumptions and then combines them together to form an inferred conclusion

We want our arguments to be [[valid arguments]]


==Laws of logic==

Laws of logic in lecture today

# Double negation: ~~A<->A
# Commutative laws: A v B <-> B v A, A ^ B <-> B ^ A
# Associative laws
[[Category:Discrete Mathematics]]
[[sn:Propositional logic]]== Further reading ==

* [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Propositional_calculus Wikipedia Article]
[[Category:Discrete Mathematics]]
[[sn:Propositional logic]]
[[Category:Pages]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
